root_text.ud1.7:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā pāvāyaṁ viharati ajakalāpake cetiye, ajakalāpakassa yakkhassa bhavane.
root_text.ud1.7:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ abbhokāse nisinno hoti; devo ca ekamekaṁ phusāyati.
root_text.ud1.7:1.4: Atha kho ajakalāpako yakkho bhagavato bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato avidūre tikkhattuṁ “akkulo pakkulo”ti akkulapakkulikaṁ akāsi:
root_text.ud1.7:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud1.7:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in Pāvā at the Ajakalāpaka Shrine, the haunt of the native spirit Ajakalāpaka.
translation_text.ud1.7:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was meditating in the open during the dark of night, while a gentle rain drizzled down.
translation_text.ud1.7:1.4: Then Ajakalāpaka, wanting to make the Buddha feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, approached him, and while not far away yelled three times, “Grr! Arrgh!” Then he added,
translation_text.ud1.7:1.5: “Now that’s a goblin for you, ascetic!”
translation_text.ud1.7:2.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
